items.title: Content collection schema
items.intent: Astro content collections must be defined for series, commentary, and architecture entries with a status-based editorial workflow (idea/draft/review/published). All content lives in src/content/ with status in frontmatter — production builds filter to published only, dev server shows everything. Schema must be Zod-validated and type-safe.
steps: Create src/content/ directory structure (series, commentary, architecture)
Define series collection schema: title, number, publishedDate, description, tags, status (idea/draft/review/published), companionArtifacts (optional)
Define commentary collection schema: title, publishedDate, tags, description, status, tier (signal/architecture/deep-dive)
Define architecture collection schema: title, status, concern, patternType, tags, relatedProjects, updated
Configure content collections in src/content.config.ts with Zod validation
Implement build-time filtering: only status=published in production, all statuses in dev
Add seed entries at various statuses to verify filtering works

acceptanceCriteria.text: Content collections are defined for series, commentary, and architecture with status field
acceptanceCriteria.verify: test -f src/content.config.ts && grep -q 'status' src/content.config.ts && grep -q 'series' src/content.config.ts && grep -q 'commentary' src/content.config.ts && grep -q 'architecture' src/content.config.ts

acceptanceCriteria.text: At least one seed entry exists per collection
acceptanceCriteria.verify: ls src/content/series/*.md src/content/series/*.mdx src/content/commentary/*.md src/content/commentary/*.mdx src/content/architecture/*.md src/content/architecture/*.mdx 2>/dev/null | wc -l | grep -qv '^0$'

items.groomingNotes: No blocking deps. Content directories (series, commentary, architecture) already exist in src/content/ — FEAT-001 only needs to create content.config.ts and the Zod schemas. Build-time filtering is the key behavior: production must exclude non-published entries. Seed content already exists in commentary/ and series/. Mark ready: all prerequisites are in place.

items.title: Base typography and prose styling
items.intent: Long-form prose must render with Literata serif at comfortable size (17px), line-height 1.75, max ~720px content width, and proper heading hierarchy using DM Serif Display. Code blocks use JetBrains Mono on elevated dark surfaces with amber syntax accents. This editorial typography is the key differentiator.
steps: Choose and load a reading serif font (Source Serif 4, Literata, or Newsreader) for body prose
Define prose styles: max-width ~720px, line-height 1.7–1.8, comfortable paragraph spacing
Style headings h1-h4 with DM Serif Display, clear size hierarchy
Style inline code with monospace on slightly elevated background
Style blockquotes, lists, and links with amber/gold accent for links
Style code blocks with dark elevated surface, proper padding, monospace font
Verify rendering with existing seed content markdown

acceptanceCriteria.text: Prose body text uses a reading serif font, not sans-serif
acceptanceCriteria.verify: grep -q 'Source Serif\|Literata\|Newsreader' src/styles/global.css || grep -q 'Source Serif\|Literata\|Newsreader' src/layouts/Base.astro

acceptanceCriteria: Prose content renders with max line width under 75ch
items.groomingNotes: Added verify commands for all ACs — prose width, heading font, and code block styles are all checkable via grep. These were previously unverifiable opinion statements.

items.title: Dark mode
items.intent: The site must render dark-first with very dark blue-black backgrounds (#0a0a0f–#111118 range), off-white text, and correct contrast ratios. Light mode is not planned. Base.astro must switch from bg-white/text-gray-900 to the dark surface/text tokens. This is the default — not a toggle, not a preference.
steps: Update Base.astro body classes from bg-white text-gray-900 to dark surface/text tokens
Ensure html element has color-scheme: dark
Verify CookieConsent component renders correctly on dark background
Verify contrast ratios meet WCAG AA for body text and headings on dark surfaces

acceptanceCriteria.text: Body renders with dark background (#0a0a0f–#111118 range) and light text

items.groomingNotes: No blocking deps. global.css and Base.astro exist. Steps are clear: set dark scheme in CSS, define surface/text colors in @theme, verify contrast. AC improved — check dark color scheme is declared, not just that the word 'dark' appears. Priority confirmed must: dark-mode-first is the design intent, not an enhancement.

items.title: Navigation component
items.intent: Every page must have consistent site-wide navigation linking to all top-level sections, working on mobile and desktop with no JavaScript required for basic navigation.
steps: Create src/components/Nav.astro with links to all top-level routes
Include site title/logo linking to home
Add responsive mobile layout (CSS-only hamburger or always-visible compact nav)
Integrate Nav into Base.astro layout
Highlight current page in navigation

acceptanceCriteria.text: Navigation component renders on every page via Base layout

acceptanceCriteria: Navigation includes links to series, commentary, architecture, projects, and about
items.groomingNotes: Added verify for nav links. Corrected 'projects' to 'products' to match FEAT-010 naming. Nav link presence is checkable via grep on the Nav component.

items.title: Home page
items.intent: The home page must read as an editorial masthead, not a SaaS landing page. Simon's name, a one-line positioning statement, and three clear paths: the flagship series, the products, and the commentary. No hero image, no animation, no bento boxes. Let the writing be the thing. Latest series installment and latest commentary visible. Subscribe CTA present but not dominant.
steps: Replace scaffold index.astro with editorial masthead layout
Add positioning statement (one line, who Simon is and what he believes)
Add three clear navigation paths: series, products, commentary
Query and display latest series installment
Query and display latest commentary post
Add subscribe/newsletter CTA — present but restrained
No hero image, no animation, no gradients, no bento grid
acceptanceCriteria: Home page displays positioning statement and three content paths
No img tags or background images on home page
items.groomingNotes: Added verify for subscribe CTA — it is the primary conversion action and the hardest element to forget. Thesis/series/commentary rendering requires visual review; subscribe presence is automatable.

items.title: About page
items.intent: /about must tell the narrative arc from cloud infrastructure to AI infrastructure, state current projects, declare advisory availability, and include contact details — all on one screen.
steps: Create src/pages/about.astro using Base layout
Write narrative arc section (cloud infra → AI infra)
List current projects with brief descriptions
Add availability statement and contact details
Add colophon describing how the site is built
acceptanceCriteria: Page includes contact information and availability statement

items.title: Series landing and detail pages
items.intent: /series must show the full arc with all installments listed; each installment page must render title, number, reading time, full content, prev/next navigation, related KB links, and subscribe CTA.
steps: Create src/pages/series/index.astro listing all installments from content collection
Create src/pages/series/[slug].astro for individual installment pages
Render installment metadata: number, title, date, reading time
Add prev/next navigation between installments
Add subscribe CTA at end of each installment
acceptanceCriteria: Individual installment pages render full content with prev/next nav
items.groomingNotes: Added verify for slug page. The original AC 'render full content with prev/next nav' is too broad for automation — slug page existence plus successful build is the testable gate. Visual review covers the rest.

items.title: Commentary feed
items.intent: /commentary must display a reverse-chronological, tagged, filterable feed of short-form posts with individual post pages.
steps: Create src/pages/commentary/index.astro with reverse-chronological listing
Create src/pages/commentary/[slug].astro for individual posts
Display tags on listing and detail pages
Add tag-based filtering on the listing page

acceptanceCriteria.text: /commentary route lists posts in reverse chronological order

acceptanceCriteria: Individual commentary posts render with tags displayed
items.groomingNotes: Added verify for slug page. Tag rendering requires visual confirmation — slug page existence plus successful build is the automatable gate.

items.title: Architecture KB landing and entry pages
items.intent: /architecture must show structured entries browsable by concern and pattern type, each following the standard record template with problem, when to use, how it works, trade-offs, and related patterns.
steps: Create src/pages/architecture/index.astro with entries grouped by concern
Create src/pages/architecture/[slug].astro for individual entries
Render the standard record template fields from frontmatter
Add browsing by concern and pattern type
Show status badge (draft/working/stable) on each entry

acceptanceCriteria.text: /architecture route lists entries browsable by concern

acceptanceCriteria: Individual entries render the structured record template

items.title: Product ecosystem and individual product pages
items.intent: /products must present the full agent infrastructure ecosystem — how ClawHQ, AIShore, Easy Markdown, and Clawdius fit together — with individual landing pages per product showing problem statement, architecture, current status, quickstart, and GitHub link. Products must be data-driven, not hardcoded.
steps: Create a products content collection or data file with structured product info
Create src/pages/products/index.astro showing the ecosystem overview and relationship map
Create src/pages/products/[slug].astro for individual product landing pages
Include all four products: ClawHQ, AIShore, Easy Markdown, Clawdius
Show how products relate to each other in the agent infrastructure lifecycle
Link to relevant series installments and KB entries from each product page

acceptanceCriteria.text: /products route shows ecosystem overview with all four products
Individual product pages exist and render

items.title: Tag system with cross-track linking
items.intent: Tags must work across all three content tracks so a tag like agent-security connects series installments, commentary posts, and KB entries on a single tag page.
steps: Create src/pages/tags/index.astro listing all tags with counts
Create src/pages/tags/[tag].astro showing all content with that tag across tracks
Group results by track (series, commentary, architecture) on tag pages
Render tag links on all content detail pages

acceptanceCriteria.text: /tags route lists all tags used across content tracks

acceptanceCriteria: Individual tag pages show content from all three tracks

items.title: Newsletter signup and RSS
items.intent: /newsletter must be a compelling landing page that sells the value of subscribing — what readers get, how often, and why it matters. RSS feed must cover all content tracks. This is the primary distribution channel, not an afterthought.
steps: Create src/pages/newsletter.astro with description and signup form
Add RSS feed generation using @astrojs/rss
Include series and commentary in the RSS feed
Add RSS link to site head and navigation

items.title: Start Here page
items.intent: /start-here must orient first-time visitors by interest area with curated entry points into existing content — if you care about security start here, if you want the full thesis start from installment 1.
steps: Create src/pages/start-here.astro using Base layout
Organize entry points by interest area (security, orchestration, full thesis, etc.)
Link to specific series installments and KB entries
Add to navigation

items.title: Changelog page
items.intent: /changelog must show dated entries of what shipped, published, or changed — lightweight and low-effort to maintain.
steps: Create src/pages/changelog.astro using Base layout
Define a simple data format for changelog entries (date, description)
Render entries in reverse chronological order

items.title: Talks page
items.intent: /talks must exist as a minimal page that can grow as speaking opportunities emerge — not empty, but honest about being early.
steps: Create src/pages/talks.astro using Base layout
Add introductory text about speaking interests and topics
Include contact info for speaking inquiries

items.title: Code blocks with syntax highlighting and copy button
items.intent: All code blocks across the site must have language-aware syntax highlighting with a dark-mode-matched theme and a one-click copy button. For a developer audience reading infrastructure code, this is core readability, not polish.
steps: Configure Astro's built-in Shiki syntax highlighter with a dark theme
Add a copy-to-clipboard button to all code blocks via vanilla JS
Style the copy button to be unobtrusive but discoverable
acceptanceCriteria: Code blocks render with syntax highlighting
Code blocks have a functional copy button
items.groomingNotes: Added verify commands. Shiki is Astro's built-in highlighter — check astro config, not package.json. Copy button verify checks component/layout/style files for clipboard implementation.

items.title: Reading time calculation
items.intent: Every long-form content page (series, commentary, architecture) must display an estimated reading time calculated from word count.
steps: Create a reading time utility function (words / 200 wpm)
Integrate into series, commentary, and architecture detail pages
Display reading time in metadata area of each page
acceptanceCriteria: Series detail pages show reading time

items.title: SEO and Open Graph meta tags
items.intent: Every page must emit correct Open Graph tags, Twitter cards, canonical URLs, and JSON-LD structured data (Article, TechArticle, SoftwareApplication as appropriate) so shares look professional, search indexing is accurate, and Google rich results work.
steps: Extend Base.astro to accept and render OG meta tags (title, description, image, type)
Add Twitter card meta tags
Add canonical URL meta tag
Add JSON-LD Article schema for series and commentary pages
Add JSON-LD TechArticle schema for architecture KB entries
Add JSON-LD SoftwareApplication schema for product pages
Ensure every page passes title and description to Base layout

acceptanceCriteria.text: Pages render og:title, og:description, and og:type meta tags

items.groomingNotes: Blocked by FEAT-004 (Nav, Base layout must be stable) and FEAT-019 (design tokens must be in place). NOT marking ready. Grooming: steps are clear and specific. AC expanded from 3 to 5 verifiable checks — canonical URL and Twitter cards are distinct concerns from OG and JSON-LD, and both are checkable via grep on built HTML. Each check runs its own build; consider caching dist/ between checks in implementation.

items.title: Design system tokens and color palette
items.intent: The design system must establish a dark-primary palette with very dark blue-black backgrounds (#0a0a0f to #111118 range), light text, and amber/warm gold (#d4a853 range) as the single surgical accent color. The palette signals craftsmanship and maturity — forged, not vibed. Replace the current primary orange (#b86b3a) with a refined amber/gold scale. Define surface hierarchy (background, card, elevated), text hierarchy (primary, secondary, muted), and accent usage rules in Tailwind v4 @theme tokens.
steps: Define dark surface colors in @theme: background (#0a0a0f–#111118), card (slightly elevated), elevated (modals/popovers)
Define text color hierarchy: primary (off-white), secondary (muted light), muted (dim)
Replace primary orange scale with amber/gold accent scale centered on #d4a853
Define accent usage rules: links, CTAs, active states, code syntax highlights — sparingly
Define border/divider treatments for dark surfaces (subtle, low-contrast)
Document token decisions in CSS comments

acceptanceCriteria.text: Tailwind theme defines dark surface tokens, text hierarchy, and amber/gold accent scale

items.groomingNotes: No blocking deps. Tailwind v4 uses @theme in CSS — no tailwind.config.js. The existing global.css already has a primary color token for #b86b3a but lacks full surface/text hierarchy. This item defines the complete dark-mode token system that all subsequent components will reference. Replacing weak AC (grep for 'surface' OR 'background') with specific token checks per category.

items.title: Responsive layout and container system
items.intent: The site must have a mobile-first responsive container system with ~720px max prose width for content pages and ~1280px max for index/grid pages. Generous whitespace. No horizontal scroll at any viewport. Content breathes on desktop, reflows cleanly on mobile.
steps: Define prose container: max-width ~720px, centered, responsive padding
Define wide container: max-width ~1280px for index pages and product grids
Set up responsive breakpoints for mobile (320px+), tablet (768px+), desktop (1024px+)
Apply containers in Base layout or as utility classes
Ensure generous whitespace between sections (consistent spacing scale)
acceptanceCriteria: Prose container max-width is approximately 720px
No horizontal scrollbar at 320px viewport width
items.groomingNotes: No blocking deps. Base.astro and global.css exist. Steps are clear and self-contained — define container utility, apply to Base layout, set responsive breakpoints. The 'no horizontal scrollbar at 320px' and 'readable max-width' AC are visual and cannot be automated; added verifiable AC checking container class and breakpoints exist in markup/CSS. Priority confirmed must: every page inherits this.

items.title: Footer component
items.intent: Every page must have a consistent footer with copyright, navigation links, RSS feed link, and contact — completing the page frame alongside the nav header.
steps: Create src/components/Footer.astro
Include copyright with current year
Add links to key pages and RSS feed
Add contact email or link
Integrate Footer into Base.astro layout

acceptanceCriteria.text: Footer renders on every page via Base layout

items.groomingNotes: No blocking deps. Base.astro exists and is the correct integration point. Steps are clear: create Footer.astro with copyright, nav links, RSS link, contact; import in Base.astro. AC already has verify commands (grep Footer in Base.astro, npm run build). Mark ready.

items.title: Client-side full-text search
items.intent: The site must have Pagefind-powered static search across all content tracks — series, commentary, architecture KB, and products — with a keyboard-triggered overlay (Cmd/Ctrl+K). Build-time index, zero runtime JS cost until activated.
steps: Install and configure @pagefind/default-ui or pagefind
Configure Pagefind to index all content pages at build time
Create search UI component with Cmd/Ctrl+K keyboard shortcut
Display results grouped by content track with highlighted matches
Style search overlay to match dark theme with amber accents
Add search trigger to navigation
acceptanceCriteria: Search is accessible via Cmd/Ctrl+K from every page
Search returns results from all content tracks

acceptanceCriteria.text: Build succeeds with Pagefind index generated

items.groomingNotes: Downgraded must → should. Full-text search is a feature enhancement, not skeleton. A personal site can launch and serve readers without it. It depends on 4 prior features (001, 007, 008, 009) being complete — validate the site works first, then add discovery features.

items.title: XML sitemap
items.intent: The site must generate a valid XML sitemap at /sitemap.xml covering all pages and content so search engines can discover and index every page.
steps: Install and configure @astrojs/sitemap integration
Set site URL in astro.config.mjs
Verify all routes appear in generated sitemap

acceptanceCriteria.verify: npm run build && test -f dist/sitemap-index.xml || test -f dist/sitemap-0.xml || test -f dist/sitemap.xml

items.title: Custom 404 page
items.intent: Broken links must land on a helpful 404 page that guides visitors to search, popular content, or the home page — not a dead end.
steps: Create src/pages/404.astro using Base layout
Include a clear message that the page wasn't found
Add links to home, search, and popular content
Match site design language

items.title: Companion artifact system
items.intent: Series installments must be able to ship with companion artifacts — checklists, templates, decision frameworks, comparison matrices — displayed inline and linked to GitHub, so readers get actionable tools alongside the narrative.
steps: Extend series content schema with optional companionArtifacts field (title, type, description, githubUrl)
Create a CompanionArtifact component to render artifact cards
Display artifacts on series detail pages between content and subscribe CTA
Link artifacts to GitHub repository
acceptanceCriteria: Series entries can define companion artifacts in frontmatter
Artifacts render on series detail pages with GitHub links

items.title: Performance budget and Lighthouse validation
items.intent: Every page must score 95+ on all four Lighthouse categories (Performance, Accessibility, Best Practices, SEO). Zero layout shift, sub-100KB page weight for content pages, no render-blocking resources. The site plan says fast load — this enforces it.
steps: Run Lighthouse CI on built pages and establish baseline scores
Fix any accessibility issues flagged by audit
Eliminate render-blocking resources
Verify zero cumulative layout shift
Ensure content pages are under 100KB total weight
Add Lighthouse CI check to build process or document manual check
acceptanceCriteria: Home page scores 95+ on Lighthouse Performance
No accessibility violations at WCAG AA level

items.title: Product ecosystem relationship map
items.intent: The products overview page must include a visual relationship map showing how ClawHQ, AIShore, Easy Markdown, and Clawdius fit together in the agent infrastructure lifecycle — so visitors immediately grasp the ecosystem, not just a list of unrelated projects.
steps: Design a CSS/SVG relationship diagram showing product positioning in the agent lifecycle
Show connections between products (e.g., AIShore uses ClawHQ, Clawdius extends ClawHQ)
Keep it static — no JS framework, pure SVG or CSS
Ensure the diagram is responsive and readable on mobile
Integrate into the products index page
acceptanceCriteria: Products overview page includes a visual ecosystem diagram
Diagram shows relationships between all four products

items.title: Deployment pipeline
items.intent: The site must have a working deployment pipeline — push to main triggers build and deploy to a CDN — so publishing is a git push, not a manual process.
steps: Choose hosting provider (Cloudflare Pages, Netlify, or Vercel)
Configure build command and output directory
Set up environment variables for PostHog
Wire domain (simonplant.com) to deployment
Verify deploy-on-push works end to end
acceptanceCriteria: Pushing to main triggers an automated build and deploy
Site is accessible at the configured domain
items.groomingNotes: Upgraded should → must. Deployment pipeline is skeleton infrastructure — without it the site exists only locally. Pushing to main must produce a live URL. No deployment = no launch. Pairs with FEAT-030 (CI workflow).

items.title: LLMs.txt generation
items.intent: The site must generate a valid llms.txt file (per llmstxt.org spec) at build time so AI models can discover and understand the site's content, structure, and purpose — this is table stakes for an AI infrastructure site.
steps: Create a build script or Astro integration that generates llms.txt from content collections
Include site description, content tracks, key pages, and structured metadata
Output to dist/llms.txt during build
Reference: /tmp/clawdius-site/generate-llms.mjs for pattern inspiration

acceptanceCriteria.text: llms.txt is generated at build time

acceptanceCriteria: File follows llmstxt.org specification

items.title: Site build CI workflow
items.intent: Every push and PR must run npm run build and report status — catching type errors, broken imports, and schema violations before they reach main.
steps: Create .github/workflows/build.yml triggered on push and PR
Run npm ci and npm run build
Cache node_modules for speed

acceptanceCriteria.text: Build workflow runs on every push to main and on PRs

items.groomingNotes: Priority confirmed at must. Skeleton CI — without it there is no automated quality gate on main. Pair with FEAT-028 (deployment pipeline): both are infrastructure must-haves before site launch. CI catches type errors and broken imports before they reach production.

items.title: Print stylesheet
items.intent: Long-form content must produce clean, readable output when printed or saved as PDF — developers do this constantly with technical articles. Hide nav, footer, and interactive elements. Optimize typography for paper.
steps: Add @media print styles to global.css
Hide nav, footer, cookie banner, copy buttons in print
Optimize line length and font size for A4/Letter
Ensure code blocks don't overflow page width
acceptanceCriteria: Print preview shows clean content without navigation or interactive elements
